title: Complaints procedure
body: "1. Purpose  This procedure is designed to:   set out a clear route for handling complaints about the operations, policies, and procedures of Great British Energy - Nuclear (GBE-N) ensure complaints are dealt with promptly and thoroughly and that the outcomes are communicated in a timely manner make efficient use of the information collected as management information so that lessons can be learned   2. Scope  This procedure relates to complaints regarding the operation of GBE-N. The procedure does not apply in relation to the investigation of staff grievances or harassment matters, or complaints relating to the outcome of a request for information.  3. Roles and responsibilities     Role Responsibility     Management System and Knowledge Lead Acknowledge the complaint within 5 working days, investigate the complaint and provide a response to the complaint within 20 working days.   Head of Legal Provide advice where the complaint may result in a legal claim. Chair the complaints panel to review responses to complaints whereby the complainant is not satisfied and has requested an appeal.     4. Terms and acronyms     Acronym/Term Meaning     Complaint A complaint is classified as a note of dissatisfaction with the operation of GBE-N, either verbal or written.     5. Procedure  5.1. Verbal complaints  5.1.1. It should be possible to deal with the majority of oral complaints at the time they are made, either by the person receiving them or by referring the complainant immediately to another member of staff.  5.1.2. Any member of staff receiving an oral complaint (in person or by phone) should record the name, address, other contact details of the complainant, brief summary of the complaint and resolution and send to the Complaints Coordinator email.  5.1.3. If an oral complaint cannot be dealt with immediately, the procedure for written complaints should be followed.  5.2. Written complaints  5.2.1. Any member of staff receiving a written complaint (by letter or e-mail or any other written means of communication) should scan the document and send it to the Complaints Coordinator email address.  5.2.2. A written acknowledgement will be sent to the complainant within five working days.  5.2.3. If further information is needed from the complainant to resolve a complaint, this will be requested from the complainant in writing.  5.2.4. The Management System and Knowledge Lead will then undertake an investigation by contacting or speaking to all those involved and seeking evidence.  5.2.5. Depending on the outcome of the investigation, a non-conformance report may be raised to track the actions coming from the investigation.  5.2.6. A response to the complaint will be sent to the complainant 20 working days from receipt of the complaint.  5.3. Complaints which may become legal claims  5.3.1. Complaints which have any possibility of litigation will be referred to the Head of Legal. The possibility of litigation will not prevent an internal investigation into the cause of the complaint to uncover any faults and prevent possible recurrence, but the investigation of the complaint will be undertaken in accordance with advice from the Head of Legal.  5.3.2. The complaints procedure will be stopped if the complainant indicates an intention...
